  

![](reference/figures/remstats-logo.svg)

## **remstats**

### *Computes Statistics for Relational Event History Data*

  
The `remstats` package is designed to compute a variety of statistics
for relational event models. Relational event modeling approaches enable
researchers to investigate both exogenous and endogenous factors
influencing the evolution of a time-ordered sequence of relational
events. These models are categorized into tie-oriented models, where the
probability of a dyad interacting next is modeled in a single step
(e.g., see Butts, 2008), and actor-oriented models, which first model
the probability of a sender initiating an interaction and subsequently
the probability of the senders’ choice of receiver (e.g., see Stadtfeld
& Block, 2017). The `remstats` package is designed to compute a variety
of statistics that characterize exogenous and endogenous influences on
the event stream for both types of models.

The package is part of a bundle of `R`-packages developed by researchers
from Tilburg University intended to aid applied researchers in the
application of relational event modeling. For preparing the relational
event history, `remstats` assumes the prior application of
[`remify::remify()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remify/reference/remify.html)
(available on
[CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/remify/index.html) or on
[Github](https://github.com/TilburgNetworkGroup/remify)). Model
estimation can subsequently be executed using `remstimate` (available on
[CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/remstimate/index.html) or
on [GitHub](https://github.com/TilburgNetworkGroup/remstimate)).  

### Installation

To install the package in R from CRAN, use:

``` r
install.packages("remstats")
library(remstats)
```

  

### Workflow example

``` r
# Load example data
data(history)
data(info)

# Define effects
effects <- ~ 1 + send("extraversion", info) + inertia()

# Prepare event history with the 'remify package' 
# Install with install.packages("remify")
rehObject <- remify::remify(edgelist = history, model = "tie")

# Compute statistics
statsObject <- remstats(reh = rehObject, tie_effects = effects)

# Estimate model parameters with the 'remstimate' package
# Install with install.packages("remstimate")
fit <- remstimate::remstimate(reh = rehObject, stats = statsObject,
    method = "MLE", timing = "interval")
```

  

### Support

``` r
# To view all vignettes in the remstats package
vignette(package = "remstats")

#To view all help files in the remstats package
help(package="remstats")

#To view available effects for the tie-oriented model
help("all_tie_effects")

#To view available effects for the actor-oriented model
help("all_actor_effects")
```

  

### Problems while using remstats?

Should you encounter errors while using the package, should you have
questions that are not answered in the Vignettes, or for reporting any
kind of malfunction of the package, you can open an issue
[here](https://github.com/TilburgNetworkGroup/remstats/issues).

When opening an issue, please, use a descriptive title that clearly
states the issue, be as thorough as possible when describing the issue,
provide code snippets that can reproduce the issue.

# Package index

## Remstats

Compute statistics for modeling relational events with a tie-oriented or
actor-oriented approach.

- [`remstats()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/remstats.md)
  : remstats
- [`print(`*`<remstats>`*`)`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/print.remstats.md)
  : Printing Relational Event Network Statistics
- [`summary(`*`<remstats>`*`)`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/summary.remstats.md)
  : Relational Event Network Statistics Summaries
- [`tomstats()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/tomstats.md)
  : tomstats
- [`aomstats()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/aomstats.md)
  : aomstats
- [`boxplot(`*`<aomstats>`*`)`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/boxplot.aomstats.md)
  : Plotting Relational Event Network Statistics
- [`boxplot(`*`<tomstats>`*`)`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/boxplot.tomstats.md)
  : Plotting Relational Event Network Statistics Distributions
- [`plot(`*`<aomstats>`*`)`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/plot.aomstats.md)
  : Plotting Relational Event Network Statistics Trajectories
- [`plot(`*`<tomstats>`*`)`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/plot.tomstats.md)
  : Plotting Relational Event Network Statistics Trajectories
- [`bind_remstats()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/bind_remstats.md)
  : Combine two or more remstats objects

  : psABXA

  : psABXB

  : psABAY

  : psABAB

  : rrankSend

  : rrankReceive

  : recencyContinue

  : userStat

## Actor-oriented statistics: sender model

Overview of statistics for the sender model in the actor-oriented
modeling framework.

- [`actor_effects()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/actor_effects.md)
  : actor_effects
- [`baseline`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/baseline.md)
  [`intercept`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/baseline.md)
  : baseline
- [`send()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/send.md)
  : send
- [`indegreeSender()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/indegreeSender.md)
  : indegreeSender
- [`outdegreeSender()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/outdegreeSender.md)
  : outdegreeSender
- [`totaldegreeSender()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/totaldegreeSender.md)
  : totaldegreeSender
- [`recencySendSender()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/recencySendSender.md)
  : recencySendSender
- [`recencyReceiveSender()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/recencyReceiveSender.md)
  : recencyReceiveSender
- [`psABA()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/psABA.md)
  : psABA
- [`psABB()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/psABB.md)
  : psABB
- [`psABX()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/psABX.md)
  : psABX
- [`userStat()`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/userStat.md)
  : userStat

modeling framework.

## Data

Simulted relational event history available inside remstats

- [`history`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/history.md)
  : Simulated relational event history
- [`info`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/info.md)
  : Simulated exogenous information on actors in a social network.

## Examples

Examples of simulated exogenous information on actors in a social
network.

- [`both_male_wide`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/both_male_wide.md)
  : Exogenous Dyad Attribute Matrix: both_male_wide
- [`both_male_long`](https://tilburgnetworkgroup.github.io/remstats/reference/both_male_long.md)
  : Exogenous Dyad Attribute in Long Format: both_male_long
